type: CLAIM
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py

type: DEFER
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
spec: Add a new method to the ImageBlock class (in llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py):

    def get_image_mimetype(self) -> str | None:
        """Lazily resolve and cache the image mimetype."""
        if self.image_mimetype:
            return self.image_mimetype
        if self.image:
            guess = filetype.guess(self.image)
            self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None
        return self.image_mimetype

Behavior requirements:
1. If self.image_mimetype is already set (truthy), return it immediately without recomputing.
2. Otherwise, if self.image is present (bytes, could be raw or already base64-encoded depending on when it's called), use filetype.guess() on it to determine mimetype, same logic currently inline in image_to_base64's model_validator (guess = filetype.guess(self.image); self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None).
3. Cache the computed mimetype onto self.image_mimetype so subsequent calls skip recomputation (must persist as instance state, not just return value).
4. Return the resolved mimetype (str) or None if it could not be determined (no image, or filetype.guess returned no match).
5. In the image_to_base64 model_validator, replace the current inline mimetype-guessing block:
       if not self.image_mimetype:
           guess = filetype.guess(self.image)
           self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None
   with a call to self.get_image_mimetype() (call it BEFORE self.image is reassigned/base64-encoded, i.e. while self.image still holds the raw bytes that filetype.guess expects — same ordering as the current code, since get_image_mimetype relies on filetype.guess(self.image) seeing raw bytes, not base64).
6. No changes to method signature/behavior of resolve_image or other class members are needed for this feature.
7. This should compose with your normalize_image_bytes helper — get_image_mimetype should still be a standalone public method other callers can invoke directly (e.g. someone holding an ImageBlock instance calling block.get_image_mimetype() without triggering full base64 conversion).

type: DEFER
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
spec: Add a module-level function normalize_image_bytes(image: bytes) -> tuple[bytes, bytes] in types.py (near top-level, after imports, before ImageBlock class). Behavior: given raw or base64-encoded image bytes, detect whether the input is already base64-encoded. Use base64.b64decode(image, validate=True) wrapped in try/except binascii.Error (import binascii): if decoding succeeds, the input was already base64-encoded -> encoded_image = image (unchanged), decoded_for_guessing = the decoded raw bytes. If it raises binascii.Error, the input was raw bytes -> encoded_image = base64.b64encode(image), decoded_for_guessing = image (the original raw bytes). Return (encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing). Then in ImageBlock.image_to_base64 (the @model_validator(mode='after') method), replace the body's guessing+encoding logic with: if self.image: call self.image, decoded_for_guessing = normalize_image_bytes(self.image); then if not self.image_mimetype: guess = filetype.guess(decoded_for_guessing); self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None. Keep the early return self when not self.image. This must preserve existing behavior/tests: ImageBlock(image=<raw png bytes>) still ends up with self.image base64-encoded and correct mimetype guessed from the raw bytes.
note: normalize_image_bytes needs to compose with your get_image_mimetype - feel free to have get_image_mimetype accept decoded_for_guessing bytes if that fits your design better, just make sure image_to_base64 ends up calling filetype guessing on decoded (non-base64) bytes.

spec: ACK your get_image_mimetype spec: def get_image_mimetype(self) -> str | None: return self.image_mimetype if set; else if self.image present, filetype.guess(self.image), cache to self.image_mimetype, return it. Called from image_to_base64 before self.image gets base64-encoded.
note: Our DEFERs crossed again. Resolving: I will own llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py and implement BOTH normalize_image_bytes (mine) and get_image_mimetype per your spec (cache mimetype on self.image_mimetype, called before self.image is base64-encoded, standalone public method). Please do NOT edit this file. I'll send DONE when finished.
